title: From Excel to a custom application
h1: Excel and legacy systems, rebuilt as custom software

headings.text: Excel and legacy systems, rebuilt as custom software
The moment is now. AI changed the game.
We automate what slows you down. With an AI-first workflow that delivers more, faster.

paragraphs: Time to replace that overloaded Excel or outdated application with software that scales with you, takes over manual work, and lays a foundation for AI and agents.
Multiple people in the same file. Formulas nobody dares touch anymore. Errors you only spot weeks later and versions spread accross email.
Not to scale on, not to automate on, not to connect AI or agents to.
Your Excel might still work. But it's slowing your growth down.
AI has changed the rules of software development. Digging into the logic behind a system is far easier now. On top of that, we build faster, sharper, and more affordably than ever.
What used to make this kind of project long and expensive, AI now does in a fraction of the time. Don't keep paying the price of a system that no longer fits,

Margin calculations, capacity planning, quotes, time tracking, inventory management, board reporting. What once started as a quick fix is now the backbone of an entire department. It no longer scales, the logic lives in two people's heads, and your business is falling behind.

What was enough ten years ago is slowing you down today. Sometimes a full replacement is the answer. More often, it's about smart integrations or extensions around the existing system, so your investment isn't thrown away.
Manually moving data from one system to another: work that feels like wasted time, where errors creep in, and that can easily be automated.
Procedures, exceptions, client agreements and pricing rules often live only in someone's head.
Transparent and proactive communication is our baseline. No false expectations. No half commitments. No missed deadlines. No nodding along when we have a better idea.
Let's start with a conversation. We dig into that spreadsheet or legacy software and tell you whether a custom tool makes sense.
Serious news and insights on technology and innovation. Not-so-serious random facts about we are. All in one monthly email.
